task: I found a wallet in the parking lot near my building this morning and I’m not sure what the cleanest next step is. Should I just turn it in somewhere, hold onto it for a bit, or do something else first?

turns.agent_response:
Best move: **turn it in right away**.

### Clean next step
- **Pick it up only if you can do so safely**
- **Don’t dig through it more than necessary**
- **Hand it to building management, security, front desk, or local lost-and-found**
- If there’s a **name or ID visible**, mention that when you turn it in

### If there’s no obvious place to turn it in
- Take it to **police non-emergency** or the nearest official lost-and-found

### Don’t
- Don’t post photos of the contents
- Don’t keep it “for a bit”
- Don’t use the cards or cash to identify the owner by calling numbers inside

If you want, I can give you a **30-second found-wallet checklist** you can follow every time.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right call. Turn it in right away. Don’t sit on it.

If there’s building management or front desk, that’s usually the cleanest first stop. If not, police non-emergency or lost-and-found.

I’d only look enough to see if there’s a name or ID, then stop. No posting it online, no using the cards.

That’s pretty much how I’d do it myself.
